cards.name: Roy's Station Coffee & Tea
cards.address: 197 Jackson St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Roy's Station, a converted gas station turned coffee shop. Grab a coffee and settle in for a bit before the day kicks off.
cards.why: It's a genuinely cool spot and a great way to ease into a Thursday.

cards.name: Guadalupe River Park & Gardens
cards.address: Guadalupe River Park
cards.blurb: Walk the Guadalupe River trail through downtown. It's greener and more peaceful than it sounds, and the weather today is perfect for it.
cards.why: 60 degrees and sunny is basically ideal walking weather.

cards.name: Vibe & Thrive Mini Health Fair at Science Lawn
cards.blurb: Swing by the Vibe & Thrive Mini Health Fair at SJSU's Science Lawn. Free wellness resources, vendors, and a chill outdoor vibe on campus.
cards.why: It's free, it's outside, and it pairs perfectly with the Swana Sweets stop right after.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit the Swana Sweets pop-up at the Student Union. Grab whatever looks good and enjoy it outside.
cards.why: This is already locked in and it's a sweet way to transition into lunch mode.

cards.name: The Tech Interactive
cards.address: 201 S Market St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at The Tech Interactive. Everything is hands-on and genuinely fun for adults too. Check the IMAX schedule when you walk in.
cards.why: It closes at 3 so get there right after lunch and you'll have a solid window.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Head to San Pedro Square Market for lunch. Mix and match from a dozen vendors, tacos, banh mi, ramen, whatever sounds good, and grab a patio seat while the midday sun warms things up.
cards.why: No better lunch spot in downtown San Jose when the weather cooperates like this.

cards.name: Be Ready: Be Prepared for Disasters
cards.blurb: Drop into the Be Ready disaster preparedness event. Free, practical, and actually useful stuff to know living in the Bay Area.
cards.why: It's the kind of thing you keep saying you'll look into and never do — today's the day.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening at Santana Row. Browse the boutiques, walk the boulevard, then grab dinner at Straits, Sino, or the Zinnia patio as the sun goes down.
cards.why: It's the best people-watching and dinner combo in San Jose on a mild spring evening.

cards.name: Swana Sweets at Student Union
cards.blurb: Join us for “SWANA Sweets”, a community gathering to celebrate the start of Southwest Asian & North African (SWANA) Heritage Month! We’ll have yummy SWANA desserts and snacks and a collaborative &…
cards.why: You picked this one.

cards.name: Spark60: Meditations for Mortals: The Liberating Art of Embracing Your Limits with Oliver Burkeman
cards.blurb: Looking for a spark of inspiration? Spark60 is your monthly passport to big ideas and bestselling thinkers. In this edition, hear from Oliver Burkeman .
cards.why: You picked this one.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Wander the contemporary galleries and then spend some time out in the sculpture garden while the morning light is still good.
cards.why: Free world-class art in Santa Clara that most locals somehow still haven't been to.

cards.name: Market Street Spring Jubilee
cards.blurb: Catch live music at the Market Street Spring Jubilee and grab something to eat from whatever food is out — good excuse to just hang and soak it in.
cards.why: Free live music on a 72-degree afternoon is basically a perfect Thursday.

cards.name: A Twist of Lime
cards.address: 650 Nuttman St Ste 114, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into A Twist of Lime for some afternoon fun before they close at 5 — low-key and genuinely entertaining.
cards.why: Gets you moving and laughing before you wind the day down with dinner.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the boulevard, browse the shops, then grab a table at Straits or Sino for dinner and let the evening stretch out.
cards.why: Santana Row at dusk on a warm spring night is genuinely hard to beat.

cards.name: Japantown San Jose
cards.address: Japantown, San Jose
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Okayama Kobo for fresh mochi and a pastry, then wander Jackson St and browse the Japanese grocery before the crowds show up.
cards.why: One of only three surviving Japantowns in the US and a genuinely fun morning neighborhood to explore.

cards.name: Friday Fun : Legos® Fun
cards.blurb: Join us every Friday at the Willow Glen Branch Library for a fun activity for kids. Activities may include arts & crafts, special performances or workshops, and board or video games. Note: 2nd Friday…
cards.why: You picked this one.

cards.name: Patient No More Exhibition at King Library
cards.blurb: Swing through the Patient No More Exhibition at King Library, a free art show worth a slow walk-through with fresh eyes.
cards.why: Free, it's on today, and it pairs perfectly with the artsy morning vibe you've already got going.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for lunch and mix and match from the vendors: tacos, banh mi, ramen, whatever sounds good. Grab a patio seat and take your time.
cards.why: Best all-in-one lunch spot downtown and the 73-degree afternoon is perfect for eating outside.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Walk off lunch at the Municipal Rose Garden, which is absolutely worth a visit in April when everything is blooming and the grounds are at their best.
cards.why: Spring is peak season here and it's one of the prettiest free spots in the South Bay.

cards.name: Pairin Thai Massage
cards.address: 265 Meridian Ave #3, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Pairin Thai Massage and decompress properly before the evening kicks in.
cards.why: A solid mid-afternoon reset so you actually enjoy the rest of the day instead of dragging through it.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll Santana Row, browse the boutiques, and then grab a table at Straits or Sino for dinner before the evening rush.
cards.why: The European-style strip is made for a relaxed Thursday evening when it's not too crowded yet.

cards.name: San Jose Giants vs. Visalia Rawhide
cards.address: 588 E Alma Ave, San Jose
cards.blurb: Head to Excite Ballpark for the San Jose Giants vs. Visalia Rawhide, grab a beer and a hot dog, and enjoy a Thursday night game under the lights.
cards.why: Minor league baseball on a warm spring night is one of those genuinely good times that doesn't need much hype.

cards.name: Goodthing coffee
cards.address: 532 Ramona St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a coffee at Goodthing — grab a seat, wake up slowly, and let the caffeine kick in before the day gets going.
cards.why: Great coffee and a chill vibe to ease into a solid Thursday.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Hit the Dish Trail while the weather is still crisp — take the full loop up to the satellite dish for wide-open views of the Bay and the peninsula.
cards.why: 73 degrees later means now is the sweet spot for this hike.

cards.name: Kali Greek Kitchen
cards.address: 451 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Post-hike lunch at Kali Greek Kitchen on California Ave. Order whatever looks good from the Mediterranean-inspired menu and eat outside if the patio is open.
cards.why: You'll be hungry after the Dish and this hits the spot.

cards.name: Graphic Novel Book Club
cards.blurb: Skip this — it overlaps with lunch. See above.
cards.why: N/A

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Wander through Cantor Arts Center on Stanford's campus. Spend time with the Rodin sculpture garden outside and whatever exhibitions are up inside — it's always a good mix.
cards.why: Free, world-class, and a genuinely nice way to spend an afternoon.

cards.name: 1,000 Ways to Hold: A New Work By Erika Chong Shuch
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Catch 1,000 Ways to Hold, a new performance work by Erika Chong Shuch at 314 Lomita Dr on Stanford's campus. It's a free event and the kind of thing you'd regret missing.
cards.why: Live arts like this are rare and it's literally free.

cards.name: Fish With You鱼你在一起Mountain View
cards.address: 156 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Fish With You on Castro St in Mountain View for dinner. It's a fish hot pot spot — pick your broth and proteins and let the table do the cooking.
cards.why: Hot pot is the perfect way to wind down a full day.

cards.name: Perspire Sauna Studio
cards.address: 555 San Antonio Rd Unit 38, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Perspire Sauna Studio. Book an infrared sauna session and let your legs recover from the hike while you completely zone out.
cards.why: After a hike and a festival, your body will thank you for this.

cards.name: Ccrma Presents: MediaFlock 2026 – Multimedia Art Festival
cards.address: 660 Lomita Court, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: MediaFlock 2026 is a one-day art festival featuring multimedia art pieces from CCRMA students and guest artists from other California universities. Join us on April 10th to celebrate 30+ pieces of…
cards.why: You picked this one.

cards.name: Mend with Friends
cards.blurb: Do you have... ...a stuffed animal with the stuffing leaking out? ...a shirt with a missing button? ...a hole you want to patch? ...a stain you want to cover up? Bring it to the Mend with Friends…
cards.why: You picked this one.

cards.name: Knit & Crochet Circle at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Note: This one is early morning at Milpitas Library — bring your project and hang with the knit and crochet crew for a laid-back social hour.
cards.why: Low-key, free, and a nice way to ease into the day if you're up early.

cards.name: Dakao Sandwiches II
cards.address: 72 S Abel St, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a banh mi from Dakao — fresh, fast, and exactly the kind of breakfast that actually fills you up.
cards.why: Best value breakfast move in Milpitas, hands down.

cards.name: Spring Valley Trail
cards.address: Spring Valley Trail, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Hit the Spring Valley Trail while the morning is still cool — good elevation change, nice views, and you'll have the trail mostly to yourself on a weekday.
cards.why: 59 degrees in the morning is basically perfect hiking weather.

cards.name: Milpitas Library
cards.address: 160 N Main St, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Swing through Milpitas Library to browse, decompress, and maybe catch whatever's on the community boards — it's a solid mid-day reset.
cards.why: Great spot to slow down between the active morning and afternoon plans.

cards.name: Cajun Crack'n Milpitas
cards.address: 275 Ranch Dr, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Lunch at Cajun Crack'n — get the seafood boil, pick your spice level, and go full messy with it. This is a hands-on meal.
cards.why: It's the kind of lunch you actually look forward to, not just fuel.

cards.name: Zen Haven Spa
cards.address: 1224 S Abel St #1224, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Book a treatment at Zen Haven Spa and let the afternoon drift. A massage or facial here is the move before a late night.
cards.why: You've got a 4 AM locked item tonight, so banking some relaxation now is smart.

cards.name: Great Mall
cards.address: 447 Great Mall Dr, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening at Great Mall — wander, window shop, grab a snack or bubble tea from one of the food court spots.
cards.why: Easy and low-effort before you head home to rest up for the 4 AM event.

cards.name: Cafe Nur
cards.address: 280 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Cafe Nur with a proper breakfast and good coffee before the day kicks off.
cards.why: Great food and a relaxed vibe make it a solid way to ease into a full day.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Hit the Dish Trail while it's still cool — take the loop to the top and soak in the views over the Bay and the hills.
cards.why: Perfect weather for a morning hike before the day heats up.

cards.name: 2026 Development and Political Economics PhD Student Conference (DevPEC)
cards.address: 366 Galvez Street, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Catch the 2026 DevPEC conference at 366 Galvez Street — a free PhD student conference on development and political economics worth dropping into for a session or two.
cards.why: It's free and genuinely interesting if you're curious about how economics shapes politics and policy.

cards.name: Oklava Cafe
cards.address: 205 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Oklava Cafe on University Ave — order something off the menu and settle in at a table on one of the best stretches of street in Palo Alto.
cards.why: Good food and a great midday spot to recharge before the afternoon.

cards.name: Paris, City of Innovation: 19th-century Universal Expositions as seen through the Roxane Debuisson Collection of Paris History
cards.address: 557 Escondido Mall, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Head over to 557 Escondido Mall to check out the Paris Universal Expositions exhibit featuring the Roxane Debuisson Collection — a deep dive into 19th-century Paris through prints, objects, and ephemera.
cards.why: This kind of curated historical collection is genuinely rare and worth the price of admission.

cards.name: Thai Smile Bodywork & Massage
cards.address: 467 Hamilton Ave Suite 26, 2nd Floor, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the day with a Thai massage at Thai Smile Bodywork on Hamilton Ave — book a session and let your legs thank you after that morning hike.
cards.why: After a full day of walking and events, this is exactly the right call.

cards.name: Graphic Novel Book Club
cards.blurb: Swing by the Graphic Novel Book Club to see what's being discussed — even if you haven't read the book, it's a fun crowd and a good excuse to discover something new.
cards.why: A chill afternoon hang with people who actually care about visual storytelling.

cards.name: Teaching Commons Conference 2026: Courageous Curiosity
cards.blurb: Teaching Commons Conference Spring 2026 The Teaching Commons Conference is a free, in-person event held on campus every spring that brings together people from across Stanford University around…
cards.why: You picked this one.
